understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by moving the selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' was occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, which would trigger WIN and allow progressing until the final WIN for the whole game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Actions were turn-based with a finite step budget. Movement/interaction was done primarily via CLICKing target positions: CLICK could either move the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinate if a valid path/slide existed, or switch control by CLICKing another selectable piece's current position (as seen toggling between pieces at [9, 21] and [18, 21]). Attempts to CLICK blocked/invalid destinations (e.g., [35, 17]) resulted in no movement, indicating obstacles/walls or movement constraints preventing reaching/occupying that tile. Successful completion required coordinating multiple pieces to occupy all '+' tiles simultaneously within the step limit.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by moving the selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked with a '+' was occupied by a selectable piece at the same time, which would trigger WIN and allow progressing until the final WIN for the whole game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Actions were turn-based with a finite step budget. Movement/interaction was done primarily via CLICKing target positions: CLICK could either move the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinate if a valid path/slide existed, or switch control by CLICKing another selectable piece's current position (as seen toggling between pieces at [9, 21] and [18, 21]). Attempts to CLICK blocked/invalid destinations (e.g., [35, 17]) resulted in no movement, indicating obstacles/walls or movement constraints preventing reaching/occupying that tile. Successful completion required coordinating multiple pieces to occupy all '+' tiles simultaneously within the step limit.
